method: COMMENT_EVALUATION outcome (researcher-approved 2026-06-02). asotia (G810, debauchery) is the alpha-privative of the sozo save-root; two NEW standalone M38-A findings record it as salvation's behavioural and vocabulary opposite (21342/21509 untouched). M28 reciprocal queued. Source finding 33 folded.

new_findings.notes: Adopted from DIM-039-001 (wa_session_b_findings id 33); session 20260602 comment-evaluation; standalone, additional to 21342.
new_findings.finding_text: **[CHAR-1]** E — Salvation's structural opposite has a distinct behavioural-existential axis, separate from its eschatological opposite (perishing / apollumi). asōtia (G810, 'debauchery, profligacy') and the cognate adverb asōtōs (G811 — Luk 15:13, the prodigal who 'wasted his substance with riotous living') name the dissipated, self-abandoned manner of life that is the lived negation of the saved state. Where apollumi is salvation's terminal opposite (final ruin), asōtia is its lived opposite — the un-rescued life as actually practised. The two together bound what salvation rescues from: the end-state and the way of life. The relationship is lexical/structural, not verse-level: asōtia and the sōt-salvation terms (sōzō, sōtēr, sōtērion) share no verse (0 co-occurrence across 243 salvation-term verses) — the opposition is the shared save-root negated. [Adopted from DIM-039-001; asōtia now clustered to M28, reciprocal finding to be authored there.]

new_findings.tier: T7.1.5 vocabulary term for the opposite/absence
new_findings.notes: Adopted from DIM-039-001 (wa_session_b_findings id 33); session 20260602 comment-evaluation; standalone, additional to 21509.
new_findings.finding_text: **[CHAR-1]** E — The salvation vocabulary encodes its own privative. asōtia (G810) is morphologically a- (privative) + the sōzō / sōt- save-root — literally 'un-saved-ness' (Thayer) — the dedicated lexical term for the absence of the saved condition, denoting the debauched / dissipated life. The opposition is lexical, not verse-level: asōtia and the sōt-salvation terms (sōzō, sōtēr, sōtērion) share no verse (0 co-occurrence across 243 salvation-term verses); the relationship is the shared root negated, surfaced because STEP co-located them in registry 39 (debauchery). So salvation's vocabulary names its opposite not only conceptually (perishing) but morphologically — the word for the saved state contains, by negation, the word for the lost one. [Adopted from DIM-039-001; reciprocal queued in M28.]

new_flags._flag_code_note: SD_CLUSTER (not SD_POINTER): a cross-cluster reciprocal queued for M28 via cluster_link must surface as a D2 pointer for M28 but must NOT be an A6 gating code, else it would gate the SOURCE cluster (M38) whose registry (39) it shares. Reciprocal/queue pointers always use SD_CLUSTER.
new_flags.description: Reciprocal of M38 comment-evaluation (DIM-039-001). asōtia (G810, debauchery — owned reg 39, clustered to M28) is the alpha-privative of the sōzō save-root: 'un-savedness', the lived negation of the saved state (M38 salvation). When M28 (Envy/Greed/Lust) is analysed, author the reciprocal structural/vocabulary-opposite finding linking asōtia to salvation (cf. M38-A new findings under obs 242 / 397, 2026-06-02).

fold_findings.resolution_note: Adopted into M38-A (2 new standalone findings under obs 242 / 397, 2026-06-02): asōtia as salvation's behavioural opposite and vocabulary privative. M28 (asōtia's cluster) side carried by new SD_POINTER reciprocal. M04 link was a transliteration artifact — corrected to M38,M28.
